# Implementation Plan: Phase 4 — Chat System, Chat History & User Summary

**Branch**: `004-chat-system` | **Date**: March 29, 2026 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-chat-system/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by `/speckit.plan` command. Covers Phase 0 (research), Phase 1 (design), and Phase 1.5 (agent context). Phase 2 tasks generated separately by `/speckit.tasks`.

## Summary

**Primary Requirement**: Implement core chat system enabling authenticated users to send messages to multiple AI models (DeepSeek via OpenRouter, LLaMA 3 via Groq, Mistral via OpenRouter), receive responses, maintain chat history, access previous conversations, delete sessions, and receive AI-generated profile summaries. System must support English and Arabic with RTL layout, real-time multi-device sync via WebSocket, and chat export in JSON/CSV/PDF formats.

**Technical Approach**:

- **Backend**: Extend Django REST Framework with new `chats` and `summaries` apps; implement AI routing logic to OpenRouter and Groq APIs; add WebSocket support via Django Channels for real-time message broadcasting.
- **Frontend**: Build React chat interface with sidebar session history, message area, model selector dropdown, message input; integrate i18next for EN/AR support; use WebSocket client for real-time sync.
- **Data Model**: Three new database entities (ChatSession, Message, UserSummary) with foreign keys to existing User model.
- **APIs**: Five new REST endpoints (`/api/chat/new-session`, `/api/chat/sessions`, `/api/chat/sessions/{id}`, `/api/chat/send`, `/api/chat/sessions/{id}` DELETE) plus summary endpoints.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11 (Django), JavaScript/TypeScript (React 18+)  
**Primary Dependencies**:

- Backend: Django 5.x, Django REST Framework, Django Channels, requests (for API calls)
- Frontend: React 18+, axios, socket.io-client, i18next, Tailwind CSS

**Storage**: SQLite (Phase 4) / PostgreSQL (production); three new models (ChatSession, Message, UserSummary)  
**Testing**: pytest (backend), Playwright (e2e frontend)  
**Target Platform**: Web (desktop 1920px+, tablet 768–1023px, mobile 320–767px)  
**Project Type**: Web service (full-stack: REST API + React SPA)  
**Performance Goals**:

- 95% AI responses < 15 seconds
- Session load (500+ messages) < 2 seconds
- WebSocket message delivery < 500ms (real-time sync)
- Export generation < 2 seconds

**Constraints**:

- No API calls directly from frontend; all through backend
- Message max length: 5,000 characters (enforced before API send)
- No hard user limits; relies on performance targets for scaling
- WebSocket required for multi-device sync

**Scale/Scope**:

- Support unlimited chat sessions per user (design constraint: no enforced max)
- Support unlimited messages per session
- Target ~100+ sessions, 1000+ messages per typical power user
- ~15 screens/components for chat UI + sidebar + settings integration

## Constitution Check

_GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design._

### Pre-Design Gate (Phase 0)

| Principle                       | Requirement                                                                             | Status  | Notes                                                                                     |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **I. Multi-Model Architecture** | Backend routes all AI requests through REST; never direct frontend→API                  | ✅ PASS | FR-004, FR-006–FR-010 ensure backend-only routing via `/api/chat/send` to OpenRouter/Groq |
| **II. Bilingual i18n**          | UI text in JSON i18n files (i18n/en.json, i18n/ar.json); RTL for Arabic                 | ✅ PASS | FR-015–FR-016 require EN/AR support; existing i18next hook already in place from Phase 3  |
| **III. Brand-Driven UX**        | Chat layout follows nexus brand (dark theme, volt accent, glass surfaces, modern fonts) | ✅ PASS | Brand requirements documented; sidebar + conversation area matches nexus identity         |
| **IV. Modern Tech Stack**       | Latest React, Django, Node.js; no deprecated patterns                                   | ✅ PASS | React 18+, Django 5.x, modern hooks (socket.io-client for WebSocket)                      |
| **V. Security & Privacy**       | JWT auth, environment variables for API keys, input validation, user-owned data         | ✅ PASS | Phase 3 auth reused; FR-017–FR-019 enforce validation and key management                  |

**Gate Result**: ✅ **PASS** — No constitution violations. Phase 0 research can proceed.

### Post-Design Gate (Phase 1)

| Principle                       | Design Artifact                                                   | Status  | Notes                                                                     |
| ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **I. Multi-Model Architecture** | AI router implementation, API endpoints documented                | PENDING | Will verify in Phase 1 design artifact (data-model.md, contracts/api.md)  |
| **II. Bilingual i18n**          | Chat UI components use i18n hooks; Arabic RTL layout confirmed    | PENDING | Will verify in data-model.md (entity language fields) and contracts/ui.md |
| **III. Brand-Driven UX**        | Chat component design matches brand; responsive layouts specified | PENDING | Will verify in contracts/ui.md and quickstart.md (mockups/wireframes)     |
| **IV. Modern Tech Stack**       | Architecture diagram confirms latest versions; no legacy patterns | PENDING | Will verify in architecture section of data-model.md                      |
| **V. Security & Privacy**       | Data isolation per user; input validation layer designed          | PENDING | Will verify in contracts/data-security.md and FRs implemented             |

**Post-Design Re-check**: Scheduled for end of Phase 1 (after data-model.md, contracts/\*, quickstart.md generated)

**Key Files Created**:

- Backend: 8 new files in chats/ app (models, views, router, services, tests, etc.)
- Frontend: 12 new React components + 4 service/hook files
- Config: 2 updated (settings.py, asgi.py) + 1 new (websocket.py for Django Channels)
- Tests: 10+ new test files for chat functionality, routing, WebSocket, e2e

## Complexity Tracking

| Consideration                | Why Needed                                                        | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| WebSocket + HTTP hybrid      | Real-time multi-device sync (FR-21) + immediate UI feedback       | Polling every 5s would increase latency to 5s; WebSocket < 500ms per FR-21  |
| Separate AI router module    | Support three distinct AI providers with dynamic routing by model | Hardcoding would violate Constitution Principle I (multi-model routing)     |
| Django Channels middleware   | Authenticate WebSocket connections with user JWT tokens           | Unauthenticated broadcast violates V (Security by Core Design)              |
| Dual session state (DB + UI) | Persist chat history + provide instant UI response (optimistic)   | DB-only adds 1–2s latency per message; violates SC-001 (<1s session create) |

---

## Phase 0: Research & Technical Discovery

**Objective**: Resolve "NEEDS CLARIFICATION" areas from spec; establish best practices for AI routing, WebSocket architecture, and multi-language chat UX.

**Research Tasks** (to be detailed in research.md):

1. **AI Provider Integration Research**
   - OpenRouter authentication, model IDs, request/response format (DeepSeek, Mistral)
   - Groq authentication, model IDs, request/response format (LLaMA 3)
   - Error handling, rate limits, latency profiles for each provider
   - Cost estimation per model per message

2. **WebSocket Architecture & Scaling**
   - Django Channels setup for WebSocket support in existing Django project
   - Socket.io vs native WebSocket considerations
   - Real-time message broadcasting to multiple clients (device sync pattern)
   - Connection authentication & JWT token validation in WebSocket middleware
   - Horizontal scaling for production (Redis adapter for channel layer)

3. **Chat Export Implementation Patterns**
   - JSON serialization format for messages + session metadata
   - CSV export (tabular message format for spreadsheets)
   - PDF generation (printable chat history)
   - Client-side vs server-side generation trade-offs

4. **Multi-Language Chat UI Patterns**
   - RTL layout for Arabic messages, input field, timestamps
   - i18n key naming conventions for chat-specific strings
   - Language persistence across session switches
   - Arabic-specific font rendering (DM Sans supports Arabic)

5. **Real-Time Message Sync Strategy**
   - Optimistic rendering: show message in UI before server confirmation
   - Conflict resolution: handle message send failures gracefully
   - Offline resilience: queue messages if WebSocket disconnects
   - Multi-device sync: consistency across browser tabs/windows

6. **Performance Optimization**
   - Database indexing strategy for ChatSession and Message queries
   - Message pagination for large sessions (500+ messages)
   - Lazy loading history sidebar (load on scroll)
   - WebSocket message throttling to prevent spam

**Output**: research.md with findings, recommendations, and code snippets for each area.

---

## Phase 1: Design & Architecture

**Objective**: Define data model, API contracts, UI structure, and quickstart guide.

### 1.1 Data Model (data-model.md)

**Entities**:

- **ChatSession**: id, user_id, title, model, language, created_at, updated_at
- **Message**: id, session_id, sender (user/ai), message, language, created_at
- **UserSummary**: id, user_id, summary, language, message_count, updated_at

**Key Decisions**:

- Messages retained indefinitely (FR clarification Q1)
- Summary generation every 15–20 messages, opt-out model (Q2)
- No enforced user limits (Q3)
- WebSocket real-time sync required (Q4)
- Export in JSON/CSV/PDF (Q5)

### 1.2 API Contracts (contracts/api.md)

- `POST /api/chat/new-session` — Create session
- `GET /api/chat/sessions` — List all sessions
- `GET /api/chat/sessions/{id}` — Get messages in session
- `POST /api/chat/send` — Send message to AI
- `DELETE /api/chat/sessions/{id}` — Delete session
- `GET /api/summary` — Get user summary
- `POST /api/summary/generate` — Generate summary
- `POST /api/chat/sessions/{id}/export` — Export as JSON/CSV/PDF

### 1.3 WebSocket Contracts (contracts/websocket.md)

- `message.new` — Broadcast new messages to user's devices
- `session.created`, `session.deleted` — Broadcast session changes
- `typing.start`, `typing.stop` — Presence indicators
- `summary.updated` — Broadcast summary updates

### 1.4 UI Contracts (contracts/ui.md)

- 2-column chat layout (sidebar + messages) on desktop
- Collapsible sidebar on tablet
- Single column on mobile with hamburger menu
- RTL support for Arabic
- Loading states: skeletons, typing indicators, export progress

### 1.5 Data Security (contracts/data-security.md)

- Input validation: max 5,000 chars, sanitize HTML
- Data isolation: users access only own sessions
- WebSocket: authenticated via JWT
- API keys: environment variables only

### 1.6 Quickstart Guide (quickstart.md)

- Local setup, database migrations, start backend/frontend
- First chat creation, message send, WebSocket testing
- Test multi-device sync by opening chat in two tabs

**Output Artifacts**: research.md, data-model.md, 4 contract files, quickstart.md
